prompt:
A simple DC-blocking filter is required. It must pass signals at 7.61 kHz with less than 1.5 dB attenuation while rejecting frequencies at 70.2 Hz by at least 30.1 dB. The circuit must use only passive components and should be as low-cost as possible.

REQUIREMENTS:
  Type: high-pass filter
  Passband edge (fc): 7.61 kHz  [max loss: 1.5 dB]
  Stopband edge (fs): 70.23 Hz  [min attenuation: 30.1 dB]
ground_truth_netlist:
* Single-stage passive RC high-pass filter
V1   VIN   0     AC 1

C1   VIN    VOUT   2.25n
R1   VOUT   0      28k

iterations.cot_reasoning:
══ STEP 1 — DETERMINE FILTER ORDER ══
I need a 1st-order high-pass filter.

══ STEP 2 — COMPUTE COMPONENT VALUES ══
First, verify the stopband attenuation requirement:
\[ A(fs) = 10 \log_{10}(1 + (\frac{fc}{fs})^2) \]
\[ A(70.23 \text{ Hz}) = 10 \log_{10}(1 + (\frac{7610}{70.23})^2) \]
\[ A(70.23 \text{ Hz}) = 10 \log_{10}(1 + 1369.7) \]
\[ A(70.23 \text{ Hz}) = 10 \log_{10}(1370.7) \]
\[ A(70.23 \text{ Hz}) \approx 31.37 \text{ dB} \]

The calculated attenuation is greater than the required 30.1 dB, so the single-stage RC high-pass filter meets the stopband requirement.

Next, calculate the component values:
\[ fc = 7610 \text{ Hz} \]
\[ R \cdot C = \frac{1}{2\pi \cdot fc} \]
\[ R \cdot C = \frac{1}{2\pi \cdot 7610} \]
\[ R \cdot C \approx 2.02 \times 10^{-5} \text{ s} \]

Choose R = 10 k

R1   VOUT  0      10k
